reviews.review_msg: Not rly sure what to say, he's nice but didnt teach anything after the midterm. Spent all the lectures talking about his own research or our final project, but didnt release the project guidelines until dec 1. Very disorganized

Okay hear me out… I actually really liked Bryan. He is very unorganized and goes off topic on tangents but it makes the course extremely easy. He also gave us a free textbook which I appreciated. He was also pretty approachable and very funny.
He is really chill and isn't too strict with grading. The overall class average was pretty high (B+)

The most disorganized prof ever. The midterm and final were short and long answer questions of 200-300 words. His lectures were completely useless because he would go off topic all the time, so it was hard to know what was important to take notes of. The guy is nice and funny, but he was just sooo disorganized.

This was the most disorganized course I have ever taken. Brian had to delay every single assignment because he forgot to put out the guidelines. He also changed the format of the midterm 3 times including the night before the midterm. He also failed to put the final out on time (the final was take-home and two questions). Do not take this course.

he doesn't really teach anything. exams are textbook based. he is really funny (that's the only reason why i went to class) VERY easy marker. very approachable, so if you have questions, don't hesitate to ask. the assignment seems hard, but it isn't.

At least he acknowledges he goes off topic.  But overall, a medium-hard marker and a very nice, approachable guy.  The project is worth more than the midterm, no final. Better get the book, he goes off topic too much in class. He was okay.I would recommend him to ppl that prefer self-learning.

He's a very nice guy. Helpful, very clear in expectations. Only thing I can complain about is that 350 requires too much work for a 3 credit course. Bryan did not have enough time for the course or us students. He was always leaving early due to his other commitments.

Valerie is very understanding and fair. If you want to go into law enforcement, take this class as she is a sergeant for the VPD. She provides unique examples and personal experiences that are beneficial to the learner. Would highly recommend and would definitely take another class with her again.

My favorite professor at SFU. She makes boring topics interesting, is really funny, and makes the exams super easy. She explicitly said in the beginning of the semester that it's an easy class and you'll have to try HARD to fail. The class' average on the midterm was an A-

Martin was a decent prof. I did find that his course structure can be confusing at times based on what he asks for on assignments. Nonetheless, he always respond fast to emails, which is good. His lecture slides were helpful and can contribute to most of what you need to know. Exam question can straight from textbook.
Amazing prof, honestly was best part of sfu . Just show up to lecture and pay attention to citations you will do amazing in this course.

If you are a student with high value for organized and timely guidelines for assignments, clear slides and lecture style, and a professor that carries their weight/responsibilities... this is NOT the guy for you. He's not only a rambler but also... all over the place.
One of the best profs I've had at SFU. Knowledgeable, friendly, concise lectures, and clear guidelines. Definitely recommend.
She only reads her notes on class, and her notes are copied exactly word by word from textbook. You don't need to go to class.
She's awesome. Super chill class only consisted of a midterm, paper, final and participation marks.
